2604.25498.title: SymphonyGen: 3D Hierarchical Orchestral Generation with Controllable Harmony Skeleton
2604.25498.paper_summary: Generating symphonic music requires simultaneously managing high-level structural form and dense, multi-track orchestration. Existing symbolic models often struggle with a "complexity-control imbalance", in which scaling bottlenecks limit long-term granular steerability. We present SymphonyGen, a 3D hierarchical framework for contemporary cinematic orchestration. SymphonyGen employs a cascading decoder architecture that decomposes the Bar, Track, and Event axes, improving computational efficiency and scalability over conventional 1D or 2D models. We introduce "short-score" conditioning via a beat-quantized multi-voice harmony skeleton, enabling outline control while preserving textural diversity. The model is further refined using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with a cross-modal audio-perceptual reward, aligning symbolic output with modern acoustic expectations. Additionally, we implement a dissonance-averse sampling algorithm to suppress unintended tonal clashes during inference. Objective evaluations show that both reinforcement learning and dissonance-averse sampling effectively enhance harmonic cleanliness while maintaining melodic expression. Subjective evaluations demonstrate that SymphonyGen outperforms baselines in musicality and preference for orchestral music generation. Demo page: https://symphonygen.github.io/
2604.25498.paper_summary_zh: 生成交響音樂需要同時管理高層次的結構形式和密集的多軌編曲。現有的符號模型常常面臨“複雜性控制不平衡”的問題，其中擴展瓶頸限制了長期的細粒度可控性。我們提出了SymphonyGen，一個用於當代電影編曲的3D層次框架。SymphonyGen採用級聯解碼器架構，將小節、軌道和事件軸進行分解，提高了計算效率和可擴展性，超越了傳統的1D或2D模型。我們通過節拍量化的多聲部和聲骨架引入了“短譜”條件，實現了大綱控制，同時保持了質感的多樣性。該模型進一步使用群體相對策略優化（GRPO）進行精煉，並結合跨模態的音頻感知獎勵，使符號輸出與現代聲學期望保持一致。此外，我們實施了一種避免不和諧的取樣算法，以抑制推理過程中意外的音調衝突。客觀評估顯示，強化學習和避免不和諧的取樣都有效地增強了和聲的純淨度，同時保持了旋律的表達。主觀評估表明，SymphonyGen在音樂性和對交響音樂生成的偏好方面超越了基準。演示頁面：https://symphonygen.github.io/
2604.25498.author: Xuzheng He et.al.
2604.25498.authors: Xuzheng He, Nan Nan, Zhilin Wang, Ziyue Kang, Zhuoru Mo, Ao Li, Yu Pan, Xiaobing Li, Feng Yu, Xiaohong Guan
